x.title: This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what compa
x.summary:
This is a great post if youre thinking about applied AI in the enterprise. The headline of this post is about what companies have huge upside from AI, but the deepest nuggets are about what AI transformation looks like in an organization.

It’s fundamentally about changing the underlying workflow or business process. As we move from chat tools to agents, those agents actually have to be deployed against workflows, which usually span multiple functions in an org. This is a different way of deploying AI than solely rolling it out to end users. It takes much more work upfront, but the results are the things that actually drive significant ROI. 

“Software asks the employee to adopt a tool, but infrastructure changes the operating layer underneath the employee. The employee should still know what happened, and the process owner should still be able to pause the workflow, change a rule, approve an exception, or pull a person back in when needed. But the value should not depend on someone remembering to use the AI every day.”

The winners of this will be the platforms that can be deployed for specific workflows and business processes with a deep domain expertise. The playbook will often heavily require FDE support, change management, getting data well organized, be able to have comprehensive evals for the workflows, and much more to get right.

x.title:
The best AI builders are no longer managing every AI agent themselves.

In my new episode tmr, @imjaredz (Builder in Res
x.summary:
The best AI builders are no longer managing every AI agent themselves.

In my new episode tmr, @imjaredz (Builder in Residence at Devin) showed me how one master agent can spin up 10 sub-agents to tackle the same problem and then check who has the best solution.

→ The master agent launches and messages the sub-agents
→ Each sub-agent gets its own context to work with
→ Each sub-agent returns its work for the master to judge

📌 Subscribe to see how all this works tomorrow: https://t.co/NSTj7ijxE0

x.author: thsottiaux

x.title: I've started using html artifacts more for specs and plans, it's just much easier for me to look at than all text md fil
x.summary:
I've started using html artifacts more for specs and plans, it's just much easier for me to look at than all text md files (@trq212 was right!)

However, it's too difficult to give AI feedback to fix an html plan or even edit it directly. I have to provide it in a separate chat and hope AI gets what I'm referring to.

Instead, I want to be able to select text or stuff on the html plan to send feedback to the AI or just fix myself directly.

I think comment, editing, and collaboration features should be natively built into the harness for Claude Code and Codex web viewers. 

Would be a big improvement for human-AI collaboration.
